metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deterministic in-session verifier for the docs/llm-framework.md update in AC #6 (e.g., a `grep`/`rg` command asserting that key section headers or terms like 'project-criterion runtime resolution' and 'configuration failures' appear in the file, or a CI lint step that checks for their presence).
evidence: "docs/llm-framework.md documents the completed code-review project-criterion runtime and the focused regression suites pass; verify with .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_unified_criteria.py tests/unit/test_code_review_evals.py tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py && make lint"
ABSENCE: no doc-content check (e.g., grep, doc-test, or CI step) is mentioned that would fail if llm-framework.md were not updated
findings.finding: AC #6's documentation requirement ('docs/llm-framework.md documents the completed code-review project-criterion runtime') has no automated verifier — the supplied command (`pytest ... && make lint`) checks code correctness and style but does not confirm that the documentation was actually updated or covers the required topics.
scenarios: An executor implements all code changes and passes all tests without touching docs/llm-framework.md, satisfying the pytest+lint command while leaving the documentation requirement unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the AC #6 verify command: `&& grep -q 'project-criterion runtime' docs/llm-framework.md && grep -q 'configuration failure' docs/llm-framework.md` so the check fails if the required content is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the new `calibrate_criterion` signature (e.g., add a `gate_key='plan_review'` keyword argument) and the corresponding change to `_criteria_eval` that passes the gate key resolved by the preflight's gate-selection logic.
evidence: Plan: 'calibrate_criterion receives the resolved gate key and calls the same mapper. Existing criterion-preview/editor callers remain plan-review-only and retain the default; code-review project assets are manually authored...'
eval.py line 419: `prompt_id = criterion_id if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` — no gate_key parameter.
_llm_eval_commands.py line 158: `_eval.calibrate_criterion(args.criterion_id, repo_root=repo_root, runs=args.runs)` — no gate_key argument.
ABSENCE: no gate_key parameter on calibrate_criterion, and no spec of how the CLI's gate-selection result (plan_review vs code_review) reaches the mapper.
findings.finding: The plan says '`calibrate_criterion` receives the resolved gate key and calls the same mapper' but the current `calibrate_criterion` signature in `eval.py` has no `gate_key` parameter and always calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` (plan-review prefix). The plan does not specify the new function signature, the call site in `_criteria_eval`, or how the gate key flows from the CLI preflight's gate-selection logic through to `calibrate_criterion`.
findings.location: Scope section: 'calibrate_criterion receives the resolved gate key and calls the same mapper'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py calibrate_criterion lines 384-489; src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py _criteria_eval lines 124-183
scenarios: A code-review project criterion id passed to `rebar criteria eval` selects `gate_key='code_review'` in preflight, but if `calibrate_criterion` ignores the gate key it will call `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` with the plan-review prefix, loading the wrong eval spec path.
Without a signature spec, the implementer must infer the intended interface change — risk of incomplete wiring.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `gate_key: str = 'plan_review'` to `calibrate_criterion` and update the prompt_id derivation to `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key=gate_key)`. Update `_criteria_eval` to pass the resolved gate key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the protocol by which a project-criterion finding's `criteria` list is guaranteed to contain the LOGICAL criterion id (e.g., the runner post-processes each emitted finding to ensure it carries the logical id, or the prompt template is authored to emit it, or the `threshold_for` callback is wrapped to translate physical→logical ids before lookup).
evidence: Plan AC: 'A synthetic activated code-review project.* criterion runs exactly once in Pass 1, retains its logical criterion id, and resolves validator-safe repository prompt/eval ids.'
decide.py line 422: `block_threshold, blocking_enabled = threshold_for(f.get('criteria', []))` — threshold_for receives the finding's `criteria` list.
registry.py lines 331-332: threshold_for looks up each criterion id in the routing map, whose keys are logical ids (e.g., 'project.review-phase-boundaries').
batch_runner.py line 79: `f.setdefault('reviewer_id', prompt_id)` — only reviewer_id is set; `criteria` is whatever the LLM emitted.
ABSENCE: no protocol in the plan or code specifies that the runner post-processes findings to replace/validate the `criteria` field with the logical criterion id.
findings.finding: The plan says a project-criterion finding 'retains its logical criterion id', and Pass-3 `threshold_for` looks up the `criteria` list field of each finding against the routing map (whose keys are LOGICAL ids like `project.review-phase-boundaries`). However, neither the plan nor the existing code specifies any protocol for ensuring that a project-criterion's findings carry the LOGICAL id in their `criteria` field. The `CodeReviewBatchRunner` only sets `reviewer_id` to the PHYSICAL prompt id; the `criteria` field is what the LLM emits. If the LLM emits the physical prompt id (or anything other than the logical id) in `criteria`, `threshold_for` will find no routing entry and silently fall back to the 0.95 default — ignoring the configured block_threshold and blocking_enabled values.
findings.location: Scope section: 'retains its logical criterion id'; AC line 2: 'retains its logical criterion id'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py lines 77-79; src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py threshold_for lines 309-332; src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py pass3_over_findings lines 421-422
scenarios: LLM emits `criteria: ['code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries']` (the physical id) → threshold_for finds no routing entry → silently uses 0.95 default instead of configured block_threshold: 0.90.
LLM emits an empty `criteria: []` → same fallback.
The AC's 'retains its logical criterion id' test passes (finder output carries the id) but the Pass-3 routing test fails because the routing lookup misses.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend child AC1 (or the fan_in test) to explicitly assert that emitted findings carry the logical `project.*` criterion id and that resolved prompt/eval ids pass validator checks.
evidence: Parent AC2: 'A synthetic activated code-review `project.*` criterion runs exactly once in Pass 1, **retains its logical criterion id**, and resolves validator-safe repository prompt/eval ids.'

Child AC1: 'An offline full-gate test activates a synthetic code-review `project.*` criterion and proves its project prompt runs once across same-runner `round_a` and `round_b` requests; verify with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k fan_in`'
The child AC1 verify command uses only `-k fan_in`; no child AC uses a test key that would assert logical-id presence in findings
'Resolves validator-safe repository prompt/eval ids' is also not an explicit assertion in child AC1
container pairing: parent + 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8
findings.finding: Parent AC2 requires that a synthetic project criterion 'retains its logical criterion id' in findings — a distinct observable outcome — but child AC1 only asserts that the project prompt 'runs once across same-runner round_a and round_b requests'; neither child AC1 nor any other child AC explicitly tests or asserts logical-id retention on emitted findings. This is a partial-without-remainder gap.
scenarios: The fan_in test passes (proves single execution) but the emitted finding carries the physical prompt id instead of the logical `project.*` id, silently breaking downstream criterion-id-keyed routing lookups
The validator-safe prompt/eval id assertion is missing, so a malformed mapping that still executes once would satisfy the child AC but fail the parent criterion
findings.suggested_fix: Add an assertion to `test_code_review_project_criteria.py::test_fan_in` (or a new `test_fan_in_logical_id` case) that inspects the criterion_id field of returned findings and confirms it matches the logical `project.<name>` form, plus a check that the resolved prompt id matches the expected sanitized physical form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit acceptance criterion and test case to child 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8 AC4 asserting that a dual-gate (plan-review + code-review) logical id is rejected with an ambiguous-gate error.
evidence: Parent AC5: 'rejects unknown/inactive/**dual-gate ambiguous** ids; verify with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k cli`'
Child AC4: 'unknown or inactive ids are rejected' — dual-gate case omitted
Child scope: 'a dual-gate match is an explicit ambiguity error' — described only in prose, not in any AC assertion

Child implementation notes: 'Eval recognition must invert the same sanitized mapping and confirm the logical id is active in the repository-effective registry' — dual-gate path implied but untested in AC
container pairing: parent + 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8
findings.finding: Parent AC5 requires that `rebar criteria eval` 'rejects … dual-gate ambiguous ids' as a verifiable outcome, but child AC4 names only 'unknown or inactive ids are rejected' in its testable assertion; the dual-gate ambiguous-id rejection — an explicit separate error path described in both parent and child scope prose — is absent from the child's acceptance criteria and therefore from the verify command `-k 'eval or cli'`.
scenarios: A criterion id that is active in both plan-review and code-review registries silently selects one gate (likely plan-review default) rather than emitting the required ambiguity error, with no failing test to catch it
The `-k cli` test suite passes without ever exercising the two-matches path, leaving the error branch dead code
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test `test_cli_dual_gate_ambiguous_id` in `test_code_review_project_criteria.py` that registers a synthetic id in both registries and asserts the CLI preflight raises/returns the explicit ambiguity error; update child AC4 to name this case alongside unknown/inactive rejection.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'blocking posture' to the `code_review_decide` implementation paragraph in Scope, naming the registry call (e.g. `registry.blocking_criteria(routing)`) alongside the threshold and nit-suppression calls so AC#3's three-property assertion is fully specified.
evidence: Scope: 'Bind the kernel's threshold callback to registry.threshold_for(criterion, routing) and call registry.nit_suppressed_criteria(routing) so both deterministic decision inputs use the same repository-effective map'
AC#3: 'Pass 3 applies repository-effective threshold, blocking posture, and nit-suppression routing for the synthetic id'
ABSENCE: 'blocking posture' / blocking-criterion lookup never mentioned in Scope
findings.finding: 'Blocking posture' appears as a verified routing property in AC#3 but is not named or described in the Scope's code_review_decide paragraph, which explicitly lists only threshold (registry.threshold_for) and nit-suppression (registry.nit_suppressed_criteria). The implementation paragraph therefore under-specifies one of the three routing inputs that the acceptance criterion will test.
scenarios: An implementer reads the Scope and wires up threshold + nit-suppression but omits the blocking-posture lookup; AC#3 then fails on the 'blocking posture' assertion with no description to guide the fix.

A reviewer auditing the Scope cannot confirm that blocking posture is covered by any described API call, producing ambiguity about whether a third registry call (e.g. registry.blocking_criteria(routing)) is required.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the sentence to: '…Bind the kernel's threshold callback to `registry.threshold_for(criterion, routing)`, resolve blocking posture via `registry.blocking_criteria(routing)`, and call `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria(routing)` so all three deterministic decision inputs use the same repository-effective map.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a named constant (e.g., `ROUND_A_STEP_ID = "round_a"`) or use the canonical step-id enum/registry value for the fan-in gate, and acknowledge this string coupling in the plan so a future workflow rename triggers an explicit update rather than a silent breakage.
evidence: Plan text: 'injects activated project prompts only when `req.step_id == "round_a"`, deduplicates their physical prompt ids against the included static criteria'
ABSENCE: no named constant (e.g., ROUND_A_STEP_ID) is specified, and no coupling acknowledgment or mitigation (interface/enum) is mentioned for this string dependency
The plan elsewhere introduces CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX as a named constant, but applies no equivalent discipline to the step_id gate
findings.finding: The fan-in gating condition `req.step_id == "round_a"` is a hardcoded string literal with no named constant, interface boundary, or acknowledgment in the plan. If step-id naming conventions change (e.g., a workflow schema rename), the project-prompt injection silently stops without a test catching the string mismatch.
findings.location: Scope section — 'injects activated project prompts only when `req.step_id == "round_a"'
scenarios: A future workflow schema renames 'round_a' to 'pass_a' or 'phase_1'; the fan-in silently injects nothing; no test catches it because the test submits a request named 'round_a' matching the hardcode
A second batch step is added whose step_id happens to start with 'round_a'; project prompts are incorrectly injected into it
findings.suggested_fix: Add `ROUND_A_STEP_ID = "round_a"` to the relevant constants module and reference it in both the runner condition and the test fixture, so a step-id rename produces an explicit test failure rather than silent non-injection.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the fan-in guard uses `req.step_id == 'round_a'` as a stateless discriminant (no instance-level flag) so the runner is safe to reuse across multiple review executions; if an instance-level flag is intended, document that the runner is constructed per review execution (not shared across reviews).
evidence: Plan: 'deduplicates their physical prompt ids against the included static criteria, records that fan-in has occurred, and never injects them into round_b'
batch_runner.py: CodeReviewBatchRunner stores only _context and _context_overrides — no per-run state today

The step_id discriminant (req.step_id == 'round_a') is already sufficient to gate injection without instance-level state; the 'records' phrasing introduces a stateful guard whose lifecycle is unspecified
findings.finding: The plan says the CodeReviewBatchRunner 'records that fan-in has occurred' to prevent round_b injection, implying instance-level mutable state on the shared runner object. If the runner instance is reused across multiple sequential review executions within the same process (e.g., a long-lived server or a test that constructs the runner once and calls it for multiple reviews), the fan-in flag would remain set and silently suppress project criteria on all subsequent round_a calls.
findings.location: Scope section, paragraph 1: 'The single CodeReviewBatchRunner instance shared by both batch steps... records that fan-in has occurred'
scenarios: A test constructs one CodeReviewBatchRunner and exercises two review runs sequentially — the second run's round_a receives no project criteria.
A long-lived produce_code_review_verdict worker that reuses the same runner object across multiple reviews would silently omit project criteria after the first review.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the 'records that fan-in has occurred' guard with a pure stateless check: inject project prompts iff `req.step_id == 'round_a'`. The step_id is already a reliable discriminant and requires no mutable state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact placement and lifetime of the fan-in state (e.g., an instance-scoped `set[str]` of already-injected physical prompt ids populated during `round_a` and consulted during `round_b`) and confirm it is safe under the single-instance, single-threaded interpreter contract.
evidence: Plan: 'The single CodeReviewBatchRunner instance shared by both batch steps injects activated project prompts only when req.step_id == "round_a", deduplicates their physical prompt ids against the included static criteria, records that fan-in has occurred, and never injects them into round_b.'
batch_runner.py lines 29–38: CodeReviewBatchRunner.__init__ stores only _context and _context_overrides — no fan-in flag field.
ABSENCE: no _project_injected / _fan_in_done or equivalent flag exists anywhere in batch_runner.py or its callers.
findings.finding: The fan-in state — that project criteria have already been injected in `round_a` and must not be injected again in `round_b` — has ambiguous placement. The plan says `CodeReviewBatchRunner` 'records that fan-in has occurred' but does not specify where this flag lives (instance variable? BatchRunResult output key? side-channel?) nor the protocol by which the same instance, receiving the `round_b` call, consults that flag. The current `CodeReviewBatchRunner` is entirely stateless beyond `_context`/`_context_overrides`; adding shared mutable state creates a concurrency hazard if the runner is ever called from parallel workflows.
findings.location: Scope section, paragraph 1: 'records that fan-in has occurred, and never injects them into round_b'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py
scenarios: An implementer adding an instance variable `self._project_injected = False` creates a race if two review runs share the same runner instance.

An implementer using a BatchRunResult output key requires the interpreter to thread that key into the round_b call, which touches the workflow DSL — contradicting the 'no workflow DSL field' constraint.
Without a precise placement spec, two implementers could choose incompatible mechanisms.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence: 'The runner maintains a `_injected_project_ids: set[str]` instance variable (empty at construction); on a `round_a` call it populates this set with the physical ids it injects; on any subsequent call (`round_b`) it skips any id already in the set. This is safe because the interpreter is single-threaded and constructs one runner per gate run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether a missing/malformed project prompt asset is a fatal workflow error (raises a located CriteriaError, aborting the run) or a non-fatal coverage-gap note (emits an empty-findings record with a located error message, consistent with base_failure_result), and align the AC test expectation accordingly.
evidence: Plan Scope: 'Resolve project prompt/eval assets from the reviewed repository snapshot and report located configuration errors for activated missing or malformed assets.'
registry.py lines 124-134: base_failure_result returns empty findings + a coverage-gap note — non-fatal, workflow continues.
AC: 'verify with pytest -q ... -k missing' — the test could verify either a raised exception or a coverage-gap output, depending on the fallback model.
ABSENCE: neither 'fatal' nor 'raises' nor 'coverage-gap' is stated in the plan for the missing-asset case.
findings.finding: The fallback protocol for a missing or malformed project prompt/eval asset is under-specified as fatal vs. non-fatal. The plan says 'report located configuration errors for activated missing or malformed assets' and the AC says 'activated missing/malformed project assets produce located configuration errors'. The existing sub-step failure model in `registry.base_failure_result` is non-fatal (returns an empty-findings coverage gap). It is unspecified whether a missing project prompt should abort the workflow run or emit a coverage-gap note and continue — the two approaches have incompatible test signatures (exception vs. empty output with a coverage key).
findings.location: Scope section: 'report located configuration errors for activated missing or malformed assets'; AC line 4: 'Activated missing/malformed project assets produce located configuration errors'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py base_failure_result
scenarios: Implementer A raises a CriteriaError (fatal): the workflow aborts with a located error, which matches 'located configuration error' but breaks overlay-absent parity if the overlay path check is not gated.
Implementer B emits a coverage-gap note (non-fatal): the workflow continues with no project-criterion findings, consistent with the existing model but potentially masking misconfiguration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation deliverable to child 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8 (or a new sibling) covering the `docs/llm-framework.md` update and confirming `make lint` passes.
evidence: Parent AC6: '`docs/llm-framework.md` documents the completed code-review project-criterion runtime and the focused regression suites pass; verify with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_unified_criteria.py tests/unit/test_code_review_evals.py tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py && make lint`'
Child scope section: no mention of docs/llm-framework.md update
Child acceptance criteria AC1–AC5: none includes a documentation deliverable or lint check
Sibling roster has only one child; no other child covers this criterion
container pairing: parent + 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8
findings.finding: Parent acceptance criterion AC6 — updating `docs/llm-framework.md` to document the completed code-review project-criterion runtime, configuration failures, and eval entry point — is not covered by the sole child ticket 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8, which contains no documentation deliverable in its scope, implementation notes, or acceptance criteria.
scenarios: Story closes with implementation complete but llm-framework.md still describes only plan-review project criteria, leaving ADR 0017 documentation incomplete
The `make lint` step in parent AC6 verify command has no corresponding child-level deliverable to validate against
findings.suggested_fix: Either extend child 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8's scope/AC to include the llm-framework.md update and a `make lint` step, or create a dedicated documentation sibling ticket whose sole deliverable is the docs update and lint verification.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
